skill.name: rn-navigation
skill.description: Expo Router guidance for React Native apps, including file-based routes, stacks, tabs, modals, deep links, and protected navigation flows.
skill.summary: Build React Native navigation flows with Expo Router patterns.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, a sample URL, a .well-known path, and security keywords as possible threats. Review found no prompt injection, malware intent, credential access, hidden execution, or data exfiltration. The only real operational concern is optional local CLI examples for Expo deep-link testing.

low_findings.title: Documented Local CLI Commands
low_findings.description: The skill includes npx commands for testing Expo deep links. These are user-visible developer instructions, use fixed arguments, and do not run automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in a bash example and relate directly to Expo development. Risk is low because there is no dynamic input, obfuscation, or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby and shell backtick findings point to Markdown code fences, inline route names, and TypeScript examples. No Ruby backtick execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and code samples. There is no executable Ruby file, shell substitution syntax, or instruction that hides execution.

low_findings.title: Generic URL And Hidden Path Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The URL and .well-known path describe Apple Universal Links hosting requirements. The skill does not make network requests or access local hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line uses a placeholder domain and a standard web path for mobile deep linking. No fetch call, upload behavior, credential transfer, or filesystem operation appears there.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Keywords Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance flags are caused by ordinary navigation documentation terms. No cryptographic routine or host reconnaissance command appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain Expo Router descriptions, route parameters, and troubleshooting text. They do not invoke crypto APIs, system probes, or operating system commands.

content.value_statement: React Native navigation can become hard to structure as routes, tabs, modals, and auth flows grow. This skill provides Expo Router patterns that guide implementation and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains Expo Router file-based routing and layout groups.
Shows stack, tab, modal, and dynamic route patterns.
Guides deep-link setup for custom schemes and Universal Links.
Covers route params, back navigation, and common troubleshooting cases.
limitations: It is a documentation-only skill and does not include executable helper scripts.
It focuses on Expo Router, not bare React Navigation setup.
It uses generic sample routes that must be adapted to each app.
It does not replace testing on real devices for deep-link behavior.

use_cases.title: Create App Navigation
use_cases.description: Plan route files, layout groups, tabs, stacks, and modal screens for a new Expo app.

use_cases.title: Repair Deep Links
use_cases.description: Review scheme setup, Universal Links configuration, and link handling when routes fail to open.

use_cases.title: Add Protected Routes
use_cases.description: Design auth-aware redirects that keep signed-out users away from private screens.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Basic Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-navigation skill to design a simple Expo Router file structure for home, settings, and profile screens.

prompt_templates.title: Add Tabs And Stack Screens
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-navigation skill to add tab navigation with a root stack and one modal screen in my Expo app.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Deep Linking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-navigation skill to configure custom scheme links and Universal Links for player and schedule routes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Navigation Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rn-navigation skill to review my Expo Router auth flow, back behavior, dynamic routes, and deep-link handling.

output_examples.input: Design navigation for an app with login, tabs, settings, and player profiles.
output: A route tree with auth and tab layout groups.
Guidance for stack screens and hidden tab headers.
Notes on dynamic player routes and safe redirects after login.

output_examples.input: Help debug a deep link that opens the app but not the right screen.
output: A checklist for scheme configuration, route file names, and incoming URL parsing.
Likely causes for missing params or unmatched dynamic routes.

best_practices: Keep route file names aligned with the URLs users should open.
Use replace after login, onboarding, or completion screens when back navigation should be blocked.
Test deep links on both simulator and physical devices before release.
anti_patterns: Do not place tab screens outside the tab layout group.
Do not pass complex app state only through URL params.
Do not assume Universal Links work until hosted files and app identifiers are verified.

faq.question: What framework does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Expo Router navigation patterns for React Native apps.

faq.question: Can it create a full app navigation plan?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide route files, layout groups, stacks, tabs, modals, and auth redirects.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It only documents optional developer commands for testing Expo deep links.

faq.question: Does it cover deep links?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers custom schemes, Universal Links, incoming URL handling, and test steps.

faq.question: Is it useful for bare React Navigation projects?
faq.answer: Only partly. The examples are designed for Expo Router rather than manual React Navigation setup.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it during implementation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill provides patterns that Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can apply while editing Expo apps.
